title: eriogonum umbellatum var. smallianum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. smallianum, also known as Mormon tea or small-flowered buckwheat, is a plant species native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its compact growth and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum var. smallianum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried leaves and stems being commonly used for herbal remedies.
